target.title: fix(gateway): reject unknown OpenAI agent selectors
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92504.

Explicit OpenAI-compatible agent selectors now have to resolve to a configured OpenClaw agent before the gateway builds request/session state. This prevents unknown selector strings from creating phantom `agent:<id>:` routing state while keeping the existing default-agent path unchanged for clients that omit an explicit selector.

## Root cause

The OpenAI-compatible gateway accepted syntactically valid explicit agent selectors from headers or `openclaw/<agentId>` model names before checking whether that agent existed in the configured roster. As a result, an integration typo could be treated as a real agent target and continue through normal request/session handling instead of failing at the request boundary.

## What changed

- Validate explicit agent IDs from OpenAI-compatible headers and model names against the configured agent roster.
- Return a structured `400 invalid_request_error` for unknown explicit agent IDs on chat completions, responses, and embeddings endpoints.
- Preserve default routing for omitted/default selectors and valid configured agent selectors.
- Add regression coverage for the shared request-context resolver and the affected HTTP endpoints.

## Validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/http-utils.request-context.test.ts src/gateway/openai-http.test.ts src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts && git diff --check

Test Files 13 passed (13)
Tests 227 passed (227)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 95.00s
```

`git diff --check` completed with no whitespace errors.
